spec_match.rationale: The page details the syntax for loading external web fonts securely using cross-origin and integrity modifiers inside @font-face rules.

interactive.rationale: Features an interactive Hash Verification Simulator with toggle buttons for valid/tampered bytes, preset hash buttons, and a step-by-step browser trace table on verify.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Matches the parent description of stepping through SRI font stacks, comparing safe vs tampered bytes, and understanding SILENT fallbacks.

edge_cases.rationale: The matrix table perfectly contrasts Same-origin, CDN anonymous, and credentialed CORS combinations alongside their Referer implications.

references.rationale: Includes links to ChromeStatus, MDN Subresource Integrity guidelines, and CSS Values 5 specs.

design_system.rationale: Maintains full styling conformance using consistent cards, badges, tabular columns, and interactive borders.

openQuestions.title: Attempt live FontFace loading with SRI if browser supports it
openQuestions.detail: The verification panel is currently a beautiful logical simulator. We can make it even better by adding a button that actually attempts to construct and load a FontFace carrying the modifier (e.g., via CSS Font Loading API if supported, or style injection), letting the user witness the silent fallback stack in action on a live text box.

summary: A highly compliant, educational, and interactive simulation of web font subresource integrity (SRI) checks. Toggles, mock byte representations, and a detailed verification trace successfully demonstrate the security contracts, with only a minor recommendation to attempt a native load test if supported.
